_comment: CI-RUNNER baseline for .github/workflows/benchmark.yml's relative regression check. This is NOT the 60fps/reference-hardware target from PLAN.md (a 2022 MacBook Air M2 24GB and the developer's PC) -- GitHub Actions runners are different, variable-speed hardware; see tools/benchmark/README.md. Established as the MEAN of two consecutive, functionally-identical 'Refresh benchmark baseline' workflow runs at https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/actions/runs/29168526498 (mean 20.550ms/p95 24.109ms/max 26.786ms and mean 20.480ms/p95 25.589ms/max 32.530ms) -- ubuntu-latest, res://benchmarks/scenarios/large-battle.json at scale 1.0x, warmup_ticks=60 measure_ticks=300 (the CI-tuned window in benchmark.yml, not BenchmarkRunner's local 120/600 defaults). Averaging two runs rather than trusting a single one is deliberate: back-to-back runs with zero functional code difference can swing ~20% from noisy shared-runner timing alone (see #755) -- a single-run baseline risks reproducing the false-regression problem this automation exists to prevent (#748/#756). Refreshed automatically by .github/workflows/refresh-benchmark-baseline.yml on a weekly schedule; see that workflow for the refresh mechanics, or run it manually via workflow_dispatch.
